summary: Frontend-only AGB integration tested end-to-end. All 6 review scenarios PASS: /agb page renders with Section 5 (Schlackeeinschlüsse), Footer link present and navigates to /agb, Contact form blocks submit without consent with proper toast, 3D Konfigurator submit-order disabled until name+email+AGB are filled, Services mobile form shows toast 'Bitte stimmen Sie den AGB zu' without consent, Services order form shows toast 'Bitte stimmen Sie den AGB und dem Haftungsausschluss zu' without consent.
critical_code_review_comments: Services.jsx is 951 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline; should be split into smaller focused components (mobile form, save form, order form).
AIThreeDConfigurator.jsx is 658 lines — close to threshold; consider splitting per-step subcomponents.

In Services.jsx handleSubmit, the 'Bitte fuellen Sie alle Pflichtfelder aus' check runs before AGB validation. This means if quantity or images are missing, the user sees the generic field error first and AGB toast only fires after the rest is filled. Functionally correct, just noting the order.
AGB.jsx section 5 highlight relies on substring 'section.title.includes(5.)' which would also match sections like '15.' if added later; minor robustness concern.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AGB feature verified on preview URL. Notes for re-test: (1) Configurator path 'ai_generate' requires concept generation before next-step-3 unlocks — use 'partner_model' or 'upload' path for faster AGB-step testing. (2) Cookie banner appears on first load and can intercept some clicks — dismiss with 'Ablehnen' button before interacting. (3) Services order form requires selectedMetal+selectedFinish+quantity+images BEFORE AGB validation runs (so test must add a file via input[type=file] set_input_files). (4) Sonner toasts are rendered under section[aria-label*='Notifications']; text matches '/Bitte stimmen Sie den AGB.*/' (mobile uses shorter 'Bitte stimmen Sie den AGB zu').
